name: basecamp.talker_selector_metadata_probe_mcw
description: Focused proof-freeze primitive for the McWilliams nearby talker selector: prove C opens the interaction menu, t opens Talk to whom, and Enter confirms the highlighted first talker into an NPC dialogue response menu without treating follower rules navigation as proven.

steps.label: wait_for_gameplay_settle
steps.expected_visible_fact: McWilliams gameplay is loaded with the nearby follower roster visible before dialogue input.
steps.failure_rule: If the loaded screen does not OCR a known nearby follower name, this scenario cannot prove nearby talker selector navigation.

steps.label: open_interaction_menu
steps.expected_visible_fact: The nearby-creature interaction chooser is open.
expected_screen_text_after_contains: What do you want to do
steps.failure_rule: If the interaction chooser text is absent after pressing C, later talker-selector keys are wrong-screen evidence.

steps.label: open_talker_selector
steps.expected_visible_fact: The interaction-menu Talk to hotkey opens the creature talk selector.
abort_if_text_contains: What do you want to do
steps.abort_reason: still on the nearby-creature interaction chooser after the talk-to key
steps.failure_rule: If the interaction chooser is still visible, stop instead of treating later keys as talker-selector proof.

steps.label: confirm_highlighted_talker
steps.expected_visible_fact: Confirming the highlighted Talk to whom row opens Katharina Leach's dialogue response surface.
abort_if_text_contains: What do you want to do
steps.abort_reason: still on a selector screen after confirming the highlighted talker
steps.failure_rule: If the talker selector remains visible, do not treat later dialogue/rules keys as proven. This scenario does not prove follower-rules response selection.
